title: How Lipton is using local creators instead of building in-house social teams
tldr: Lipton activated local creators across six markets via Billion Dollar Boy instead of building in-house social teams — a new model for global-meets-local content. Concrete campaign breakdown of where creator strategy is heading.
angle: Why 'global creator networks' may replace in-house social teams — and what that means for content governance.

title: The AI search boom is fueling content budgets before media buys
tldr: Brands chasing visibility in AI search are reallocating SEO, content and creator budgets ahead of media spend. A real shift in how content investment is prioritized.
angle: How AI search is rewriting the content-vs-media budget split — and why content strategists gain leverage.

title: Survey claims brands which sound more human will get ahead in the AI age
tldr: New research argues that human-sounding brand voice will be a competitive edge as AI-generated content floods channels. Directly speaks to humanity over polish in content.
angle: In an AI-slop world, human voice becomes the moat — how to operationalize 'sounding human' in brand content.

title: AI disclosure: when it does and doesn’t hurt brands
tldr: Two new studies clash on whether labeling ads as AI-made damages brand perception — and one suggests a fix. Practical for anyone navigating disclosure norms.
angle: The emerging etiquette of AI disclosure: when transparency builds trust vs. erodes it.

title: Polymarket reportedly paid people to post fake videos of themselves placing bets
tldr: A WSJ investigation found Polymarket paid creators to film 1,100+ deceptive 'authentic' clips of fake wins. A cautionary case on manufactured UGC and trust collapse.
angle: When brands fake employee/user-generated authenticity — the reputational time bomb in staged UGC.

title: How to Write Content That Lands With Decision Makers
tldr: A practical B2B guide for thought leadership that moves senior buyers, not just dashboards. Tackles the output-vs-impact gap content teams face.
angle: Why your content metrics look great but buyers aren't impressed — writing for the people who sign contracts.

title: CMOs prioritize organizational influence over long-term brand growth
tldr: A Lippincott study finds marketers chasing internal influence and short-term results at the expense of enduring brand growth. Sharp read on marketing's internal politics.
angle: How internal incentive structures quietly kill long-term brand narrative — a comms-leadership problem.

title: Cannes is becoming 'a Super Bowl moment' for creators: How they're storming the French Riviera
tldr: Creators are taking over Cannes Lions, signaling a power shift from agencies to individual content makers. A marker of the creator economy's maturation.
angle: Cannes as creator showcase: what it signals about who holds creative power now.

title: Take a deep dive into the latest research on digital news consumption – with a warning about AI
tldr: Fresh research on how people consume digital news, flagging AI's disruptive role. Directly relevant to media-consumption trend tracking.
angle: What the latest digital news consumption data tells content strategists about attention and trust.

title: What is predictive media intelligence?
tldr: Sprout introduces predictive media intelligence — spotting which posts will spike before they do. A new analytics frame for proactive comms.
angle: From monitoring to predicting: how predictive media intelligence reshapes crisis and content timing.

title: Ad Council taps social media creators to 'spill the tea' on American history
tldr: The Ad Council uses creators in a casual 'spill the tea' format to make American history engaging. A campaign breakdown of format-matching to audience.
angle: Making dry institutional content snackable: lessons from the Ad Council's creator format.

title: Opportunity in chaos: How comms leaders can help teams thrive through change
tldr: Communicators' role in helping employees find clarity and purpose amid constant change. Solid internal comms and change-management angle.
angle: Internal comms as the stabilizer in permanent disruption — the underrated change-management lever.

title: 11 Tips to Extend the Life of Your Best Content Using Audio Formats
tldr: Repurposing top content into audio to extend lifecycle and reach new audiences. Practical content-repurposing tactics.
angle: Audio as a content-decay antidote: turning your best pieces into multi-format assets.

title: Which podcast genres are most popular among men and women in Great Britain?
tldr: YouGov data breaks down podcast genre preferences by gender in GB. Useful audience-insight fodder for B2B podcasting and format decisions.
angle: Podcast genre preferences by gender: what the data means for B2B audio strategy.
